termsAndConditions.title: Terms & Conditions
termsAndConditions.content:
AkovoLabs Speedtest & AI Network Intelligence Platform

Effective Date: 01 June 2026
Last Updated: 20 June 2026

1. INTRODUCTION

Welcome to AkovoLabs Speedtest, an AI-powered network diagnostic and cybersecurity-aware performance analysis platform operated by AkovoLabs (“we,” “us,” “our,” or “the Platform”).

These Terms and Conditions (“Terms”) govern your access to and use of the AkovoLabs Speedtest platform, including all associated services, APIs, dashboards, analytics tools, artificial intelligence features, and network measurement systems (collectively, the “Services”).

By accessing or using the Services, you agree to be legally bound by these Terms. If you do not agree, you must immediately discontinue use of the Platform.

2. ELIGIBILITY

To use the Services, you must:

- Be at least 13 years old (or the minimum legal digital consent age in your jurisdiction)
- Have legal capacity to enter into binding agreements
- Not be prohibited from using network diagnostic tools under applicable laws

If you are using the Services on behalf of an organization, you confirm that you have authority to bind that organization to these Terms.

3. DESCRIPTION OF SERVICES

AkovoLabs Speedtest provides:

- Soft Speedtests (Download, Upload, Ping)
- Hard Speedtests (Advanced diagnostics including jitter, packet loss, DNS latency, and stability scoring)
- AI-powered network analysis and explanations
- ISP performance insights and historical trends
- Network health scoring and benchmarking
- Cybersecurity-oriented anomaly detection (experimental)
- Diagnostic reporting for connectivity quality

You acknowledge that the Services are analytical and informational only and do not guarantee network improvements or ISP performance changes.

4. ZERO-BUDGET INFRASTRUCTURE DISCLAIMER

You expressly acknowledge and agree that:

- The Platform may operate on limited or free-tier infrastructure
- Server resources (CPU, bandwidth, memory) are shared
- Only one or limited geographic servers may be available
- Network test results may be affected by physical distance to servers
- Performance metrics may vary significantly from commercial speed testing providers

Accordingly, results should be interpreted as estimates, not absolute measurements.

5. ACCURACY AND LIMITATIONS OF MEASUREMENTS

The Platform provides network measurements including but not limited to:

- Download speed
- Upload speed
- Latency (ping)
- Jitter
- Packet loss estimation
- DNS resolution time
- Connection stability scoring

You acknowledge:

- Browser-based limitations prevent raw network-level measurement (e.g., ICMP ping, port information, router inspection)
- TCP slow-start affects early measurement phases
- Mobile and wireless networks may produce inconsistent results
- Results are influenced by server load and geographic distance

We do not guarantee that results reflect ISP-reported speeds or third-party tools.

6. AI-GENERATED INSIGHTS DISCLAIMER

The Platform uses artificial intelligence to generate:

- Network explanations
- ISP performance interpretations
- Security anomaly suggestions
- Optimization recommendations

You acknowledge that:

- AI outputs may be incorrect, incomplete, or speculative
- AI does not replace professional network engineering or cybersecurity analysis
- AI suggestions must not be treated as definitive technical diagnosis

You use all AI-generated outputs at your own risk.

7. ACCEPTABLE USE POLICY

You agree NOT to:

- Attempt to overload, disrupt, or damage the Platform
- Perform automated scraping, bot attacks, or denial-of-service testing
- Reverse engineer, decompile, or tamper with system components
- Interfere with speed test results intentionally (e.g., bandwidth throttling manipulation)
- Use the Services for unlawful surveillance or unauthorized network monitoring
- Attempt to falsify network metrics for fraudulent purposes
- Use the Platform to test networks you do not own or have permission to test

We reserve the right to suspend or permanently ban users violating this policy.

8. PROHIBITED ACTIVITIES

Strictly prohibited uses include:

- Cyberattacks or penetration testing without authorization
- Network intrusion attempts
- Exploiting system vulnerabilities
- Attempting to bypass authentication or security controls
- Using the system to monitor third-party networks without consent
- Commercial resale of raw data without authorization

Violations may result in legal action.

9. DATA COLLECTION AND PRIVACY

By using the Services, you acknowledge that we may collect:

- IP address
- Device type and browser information
- Network performance metrics
- Test history and timestamps
- Approximate geolocation (based on IP)

This data is used for:

- Improving analytics accuracy
- Detecting anomalies and abuse
- Generating historical performance insights
- System optimization and research

We do not sell personal data. However, anonymized aggregated data may be used for analytics and system improvement.

10. SECURITY AND CYBERSECURITY ANALYTICS

The Platform may attempt to detect:

- Network instability patterns
- Suspicious traffic behavior
- Abnormal latency spikes
- Packet loss anomalies

You acknowledge:

- These detections are heuristic and not definitive cybersecurity conclusions
- The system does not guarantee detection of malicious activity
- We are not responsible for undetected threats or false positives

11. SERVICE AVAILABILITY

We do not guarantee:

- Continuous uptime
- Uninterrupted access
- Error-free operation
- Availability in all regions

The Service may be temporarily unavailable due to:

- Maintenance
- Infrastructure limitations
- Hosting provider outages
- Network congestion
- Security updates

12. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY

To the maximum extent permitted by law:

- AkovoLabs shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, or consequential damages
- We are not responsible for inaccurate speed measurements or diagnostic outputs
- We are not responsible for ISP performance issues
- We are not liable for decisions made based on AI-generated recommendations

Your use of the Services is entirely at your own risk.

13. NO WARRANTY

The Services are provided on an “AS IS” and “AS AVAILABLE” basis.

We disclaim all warranties including:

- Fitness for a particular purpose
- Accuracy of results
- Reliability of network analysis
- Fitness for commercial or enterprise-grade diagnostics

14. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS

All content, systems, algorithms, designs, and analytics models belong to AkovoLabs unless otherwise stated.

You may not:

- Copy or reproduce system logic
- Republish dashboards or AI explanations as your own product
- Resell or redistribute internal models or analytics outputs

15. MODIFICATIONS TO SERVICES

We reserve the right to:

- Modify or discontinue features at any time
- Update algorithms without notice
- Change measurement methodologies
- Add or remove diagnostic tools

We are not liable for any impact resulting from such changes.

16. TERMINATION

We may suspend or terminate access if:

- You violate these Terms
- Abuse or misuse is detected
- Security risks are identified
- Required by law or regulatory authority

Upon termination, your access to Services will be revoked immediately.

17. THIRD-PARTY DEPENDENCIES

The Platform may rely on:

- Hosting providers
- Cloud infrastructure services
- Network routing providers
- Open-source libraries

We are not responsible for third-party outages or failures.

18. GOVERNING LAW

These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of South Africa, without regard to conflict of law principles.

Any disputes shall be resolved in the competent courts of South Africa.

19. CHANGES TO TERMS

We reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time.

Updates will be posted on the Platform with a revised “Last Updated” date.

Continued use of the Services after updates constitutes acceptance of the revised Terms.

20. CONTACT INFORMATION

For questions regarding these Terms, contact:

AkovoLabs Support
Operated by AkovoLabs
Clement.phoshoko@outlook.com

21. FINAL ACKNOWLEDGEMENT

By using AkovoLabs Speedtest, you acknowledge that:

- You understand the experimental nature of the system
- You accept all measurement limitations
- You accept AI-generated outputs as advisory only
- You agree to use the system responsibly and lawfully

privacyPolicy.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPolicy.content:
AkovoLabs Speedtest & AI Network Intelligence Platform

Operated by: AkovoLabs
Effective Date: 01 June 2026
Last Updated: 20 June 2026

1. INTRODUCTION

This Privacy Policy explains how AkovoLabs Speedtest (“we,” “us,” “our,” or “the Platform”) collects, uses, stores, processes, and protects your personal information when you use our Services.

We comply with applicable data protection laws, including:

- The General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) (EU Regulation 2016/679)
- The Protection of Personal Information Act (POPIA) (South Africa, Act 4 of 2013)

By using our Services, you acknowledge that your information will be processed in accordance with this Privacy Policy.

2. DATA CONTROLLER INFORMATION

The data controller responsible for your personal information is:

AkovoLabs
Operated by AkovoLabs
Email: Clement.phoshoko@outlook.com
Website: www.clementphoshoko.co.za

If you are in the EU, you may also contact your local Data Protection Authority.

3. INFORMATION WE COLLECT

We collect the following categories of data:

3.1 Technical and Device Data

When you use the Platform, we automatically collect:

- IP address
- Browser type and version
- Device type (mobile, desktop, tablet)
- Operating system
- Screen resolution
- Network type (WiFi, mobile data, ethernet)
- Time zone and locale settings

3.2 Network Performance Data

To provide speedtest functionality, we collect:

- Download speed measurements
- Upload speed measurements
- Latency (ping results)
- Jitter values
- Packet loss estimates
- DNS resolution times
- Connection stability scores
- Server response timing data

3.3 Usage Data

We collect information about how you interact with the system:

- Pages visited
- Test initiation timestamps
- Feature usage (soft vs hard speedtest)
- Navigation patterns
- Session duration
- Error logs and diagnostics

3.4 Location Data (Approximate)

We may infer approximate location using IP geolocation to:

- Select nearest test server
- Improve measurement accuracy
- Generate regional analytics

We do NOT collect precise GPS location.

3.5 AI Interaction Data

When you use AI-powered features, we may collect:

- Input queries
- Generated diagnostic outputs
- Network interpretation results
- System recommendations

4. LEGAL BASIS FOR PROCESSING (GDPR)

We process your personal data under the following legal bases:

- Consent – when you accept use of cookies or analytics
- Legitimate interest – to operate, secure, and improve the Platform
- Contractual necessity – to deliver speedtest and analytics services
- Legal obligation – where required by law enforcement or regulation

5. PURPOSE OF DATA COLLECTION

We use collected data to:

- Provide speed testing services
- Measure network performance metrics
- Generate AI-driven insights
- Detect anomalies and suspicious network behavior
- Improve system accuracy and reliability
- Generate historical analytics dashboards
- Improve server routing and performance estimation
- Prevent abuse, fraud, and system attacks
- Conduct aggregated statistical research

6. COOKIES AND TRACKING TECHNOLOGIES

We use cookies and similar technologies for:

- Session management
- User authentication (if applicable)
- Analytics tracking
- Performance monitoring
- Security enforcement

Types of Cookies:
- Essential Cookies – required for system operation
- Analytics Cookies – used to understand usage patterns
- Performance Cookies – improve speedtest accuracy
- Security Cookies – prevent abuse and attacks

You may disable cookies in your browser, but some features may not function properly.

7. DATA SHARING AND DISCLOSURE

We do NOT sell personal data.

However, we may share data in the following cases:

7.1 Service Providers

We may share data with:

- Hosting providers
- Cloud infrastructure services
- Analytics tools
- Security monitoring systems

These providers are contractually bound to protect your data.

7.2 Legal Requirements

We may disclose data if required to:

- Comply with legal obligations
- Respond to court orders
- Cooperate with law enforcement
- Prevent fraud or cybercrime

7.3 Aggregated Data

We may share anonymized and aggregated data such as:

- ISP performance trends
- Regional network statistics
- Global speed benchmarks

This data cannot identify individual users.

8. INTERNATIONAL DATA TRANSFERS (GDPR COMPLIANCE)

Your data may be transferred and processed outside your country, including jurisdictions that may not have equivalent data protection laws.

We ensure safeguards such as:

- Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs)
- Encryption in transit and at rest
- Secure infrastructure providers

9. DATA RETENTION

We retain data only as long as necessary:

- Network test data: up to 12–24 months
- Logs: up to 90–180 days
- Aggregated analytics: may be stored indefinitely (anonymized)
- User identifiers: removed or anonymized when no longer needed

When data is no longer required, it is securely deleted or anonymized.

10. DATA SECURITY

We implement industry-standard security measures including:

- HTTPS encryption (TLS 1.2+)
- Firewall protection
- Rate limiting
- Secure API authentication
- Database access control
- Logging and intrusion detection

However, no system is 100% secure, and we cannot guarantee absolute protection.

11. YOUR RIGHTS (GDPR + POPIA)

Depending on your jurisdiction, you have the right to:

Access

Request copies of your personal data.

Correction

Request correction of inaccurate or incomplete data.

Deletion (“Right to be Forgotten”)

Request deletion of your personal data under certain conditions.

Restriction

Request restriction of processing.

Objection

Object to processing based on legitimate interest.

Portability (GDPR)

Request transfer of your data in a structured format.

POPIA Rights (South Africa)
- Right to be informed
- Right to access personal information
- Right to correct or delete data
- Right to object to processing
- Right to lodge complaints with the Information Regulator (South Africa)

12. CHILDREN’S PRIVACY

The Platform is not intended for children under 13 years old (or applicable digital consent age).

We do not knowingly collect data from children. If discovered, such data will be deleted immediately.

13. AUTOMATED DECISION-MAKING & AI PROCESSING

The Platform uses AI systems to:

- Interpret network performance
- Generate diagnostic explanations
- Detect anomalies
- Produce scoring systems

These systems:

- Do not make legally binding decisions
- Do not determine eligibility for services
- Should not be used as sole decision-making tools

All AI outputs are advisory.

14. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY

We are not responsible for:

- ISP-related performance issues
- Inaccurate speedtest results due to network conditions
- AI misinterpretations
- Temporary service interruptions
- Third-party infrastructure failures

Use of the Platform is at your own risk.

15. THIRD-PARTY LINKS AND SERVICES

The Platform may contain links or integrations to third-party services.

We are not responsible for:

- Their privacy practices
- Their content
- Their data handling policies

We encourage users to review third-party policies separately.

16. POPIA COMPLIANCE (SOUTH AFRICA SPECIFIC)

In compliance with POPIA:

- We process personal information lawfully and transparently
- We collect only necessary data
- We secure data against unauthorized access
- We allow users to request correction or deletion
- We ensure accountability in data processing

You may lodge complaints with the Information Regulator (South Africa) if you believe your rights have been violated.

17. GDPR COMPLIANCE (EU USERS)

For EU users:

We comply with GDPR principles of:

- Lawfulness, fairness, transparency
- Purpose limitation
- Data minimization
- Accuracy
- Storage limitation
- Integrity and confidentiality
- Accountability

You may contact your local supervisory authority if unsatisfied with our response to a request.

18. CHANGES TO THIS PRIVACY POLICY

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time.

Changes will be posted on this page with an updated “Last Updated” date.

Continued use of the Platform constitutes acceptance of the revised policy.

19. CONTACT INFORMATION

For privacy-related inquiries, requests, or complaints:

AkovoLabs Privacy Office
Operated by AkovoLabs
Email: Clement.phoshoko@outlook.com
Support: www.clementphoshoko.co.za/contact

20. FINAL STATEMENT

By using AkovoLabs Speedtest, you acknowledge that:

- You understand how your data is collected and processed
- You agree to the use of AI-driven analytics
- You accept cross-border data processing
- You consent to necessary cookies and technical measurements required for service functionality
